title: Conjecture 1.1
statement_text: If $\alpha(G) \leq 2$, there is a clique cover of size at most $|G|$.
context_text: Described in the paper as 'long-standing' with unknown original source; Seymour recalls working on it possibly in the 1980s. It is used to characterise which graphs $H$ have the property that every $H$-free graph admits a clique cover of size at most $|G|$, namely exactly those $H$ that are induced subgraphs of $K_{1,3}$.
paper_contribution: The paper makes no direct contribution to this conjecture; it is discussed as motivation and context for the main results.
notes: Original source unknown; the paper explicitly states 'we do not know the source' and that Seymour recalls working on it, possibly in the 1980s.

title: Javadi–Hajebi Question
statement_text: Do all graphs $G$ with $\alpha(G)$ at most a constant admit clique covers of size $O(|G|)$?
context_text: Javadi and Hajebi [4] asked whether bounding the stability number by an absolute constant is sufficient to guarantee a linear-size clique cover. The paper addresses this directly, disproving it.
paper_contribution: Disproves the question via Theorem 1.3: there exist graphs on $n$ vertices with $\alpha(G) \leq 3$ that require $\Omega(n^{6/5}/(\log n)^2)$ cliques in any clique cover.
notes: Stated in running prose without a labelled theorem environment; attributed inline to Javadi and Hajebi [4].

title: Conjecture 1.5 (Hajebi)
statement_text: For every integer $t \geq 1$ there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that every $K_{t,t}$-free graph $G$ has a clique cover of size $O(|G|^{2-\varepsilon})$.
context_text: Proposed by Sepehr Hajebi [3], this conjecture asks whether forbidding a complete bipartite graph $K_{t,t}$ as an induced subgraph forces a strictly subquadratic clique cover. It is the central conjecture studied in the paper.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full via Theorem 1.6: every $K_{s,t}$-free graph $G$ with sufficiently many vertices admits a clique cover of size at most $\frac{3}{2}|G|^{2-1/(s+t)}$.
